core_claim: Observer entropy, horizon/overlap, MI/CMI/I3, erasure-correctability, and survivor fixed-point shadows do not determine observer reconstruction algebra or channel algebra semantics in the balanced-bridge observer family.
positive_boundary_claim: For k=1 stabilizer/OA-QEC all-region atlases, all-region erasure-correctability plus survivor fixed-point booleans determine every all-region observer-algebra signature.

diagnostic_tiers.description: Named entropy, overlap, MI/CMI/I3, shared-horizon entropy, and shared-horizon algebra.
Adds erasure correctability and survivor fixed-point booleans.
Adds exact finite logical-probe visibility responses.

proof_obligations.claim: The fast Goal 4 certificate emits the theorem-style claim, balanced-bridge prefix records, tomography tiers, and expert-facing interpretation.
Named observer entropy, horizon/overlap, MI/CMI/I3, and shared-horizon algebra agree while observer reconstruction differs.
Erasure correctability and survivor fixed-point shadows agree while erasure-channel algebra semantics differ.
The strict-cover bounded audit separates certified observer-patch hits from entropy near-misses rejected by erasure semantics.
The Goal 3 finite holographic-code witness matches entropy and finite min-cut diagnostics while reconstruction/channel diagnostics split.
The Goal 4 certificate can optionally run the Goal 3 min-cut audit live.
The bounded all-region scan over n<=4, k=1 stabilizer-code representatives finds low-order entropy non-identifiability and no collisions after adding all-region erasure/fixed-point data.
Exact k=1 all-region lemma: erasure-correctability and survivor fixed-point data determine region-algebra signatures; the command audits the implementation over the declared bounded representatives.

expert_facing_interpretation: S_Ob sets a size scale, but the observer's effective physics is specified by entropy scale, observer algebra, and channel/coarse-graining data. Entropy, horizon, min-cut, and fixed-point shadows alone are incomplete finite invariants.
